description: Canonical governed error taxonomy for spectrum-systems evaluation, feedback, observability, and regression pipelines.

error_families.description: Errors caused by poor-quality, incomplete, or missing input artifacts before any AI processing occurs.

subtypes.description: Transcript is garbled, truncated, or contains too many transcription errors to support accurate extraction.
examples: Transcript contains repeated [inaudible] segments covering key decisions
ASR output has >30% word error rate in technical sections

subtypes.description: Slides are unreadable, low-resolution, or lack sufficient text for grounding.
examples: Slide deck is a scanned image with no OCR layer
Slides contain only diagrams with no supporting text

subtypes.description: Required context (prior meeting notes, reference documents, policy) was not provided as input.
examples: Action items reference prior decisions not present in the context bundle
Regulatory citations cannot be verified without the referenced statute

error_families.description: Errors in structured information extraction from input artifacts.

subtypes.description: A decision that was explicitly made in the meeting or document was not extracted.
examples: Board voted to defer the proposal; extraction shows no decision
Go/no-go decision mentioned at minute 42 not present in output

subtypes.description: An action item explicitly assigned in the source was not extracted.
examples: Action to send cost analysis to CFO by Friday not captured
Follow-up call with vendor not listed in action items

subtypes.description: An item was extracted that does not correspond to any decision, action, or fact in the source.
examples: Extracted decision 'Approve budget increase' when only a discussion occurred
Action item assigned to person not present in the meeting

subtypes.description: The extracted span is correct in substance but incorrectly bounded, merging separate items or splitting a single one.
examples: Two separate decisions merged into one extracted item
Single action item split into two overlapping entries

error_families.description: Errors in AI reasoning, inference, and analytical conclusions drawn from input artifacts.

subtypes.description: A conclusion or inference drawn from the evidence is logically unsound.
examples: Inferred approval from a tentative agreement that was later retracted
Concluded risk is low despite explicit risk flag in transcript

subtypes.description: A contradiction between two claims or between a claim and the source was not detected.
examples: Output claims both 'approved' and 'deferred' for the same item without flagging conflict
Slide states deadline is Q3; transcript states Q4; conflict not surfaced

subtypes.description: A significant missing piece of reasoning, evidence, or context was not identified.
examples: Analysis does not flag absence of regulatory sign-off
Gap in timeline between milestone 2 and 3 not surfaced

subtypes.description: The conclusion is more definitive or certain than the supporting evidence warrants.
examples: Summary states 'team agreed unanimously' when vote was 3-2
Risk assessment concludes 'no risk identified' from inconclusive data

error_families.description: Errors where claims or outputs cannot be traced to authoritative source artifacts.

subtypes.description: A claim has no upstream reference to any source artifact.
examples: Claim asserts a cost figure with no citation to transcript or slide
Decision attributed to no speaker or document

subtypes.description: A claim has a reference, but the referenced artifact does not exist or cannot be resolved.
examples: Pass references slide_id='slide-99' which does not exist in the deck
Transcript timestamp reference points outside the transcript duration

subtypes.description: A reference exists but provides only weak or indirect support for the claim.
examples: Claim uses a passing mention as support for a strong assertion
Referenced slide contains tangentially related data, not the cited figure

subtypes.description: A claim cannot be traced to any source despite a reference being provided; severe form of grounding failure.
examples: All provided upstream refs are missing and no alternative evidence exists
Claim contradicts all available source material

error_families.description: Errors where AI output violates the expected output schema or contract.

subtypes.description: The AI output is not valid JSON or otherwise unparseable.
examples: Model returned plain text instead of required JSON
JSON output has unmatched brackets

subtypes.description: A required field specified in the schema is absent from the output.
examples: Output missing required 'decisions' array
Action item record missing required 'owner' field

subtypes.description: A field is present but has the wrong type (e.g. string where array expected).
examples: 'confidence' field returned as string '0.8' instead of number 0.8
'items' field returned as a single object instead of an array

error_families.description: Errors where AI output contains content invented with no basis in the input artifacts.

subtypes.description: A factual assertion is made that has no basis in any provided source artifact.
examples: Output states 'regulatory approval granted' when no such event is in the sources
Budget figure cited that does not appear in any slide or transcript

subtypes.description: A specific detail (name, date, number, attribution) is fabricated and does not appear in sources.
examples: Action item attributed to 'Dr. Smith' who did not attend the meeting
Cost figure of $2.3M cited when actual figure in slides is $1.8M

error_families.description: Errors detected by comparing a candidate run against a stored baseline, indicating quality degradation.

subtypes.description: Structural F1 score has dropped below the regression threshold compared to baseline.
examples: Structural F1 dropped from 0.92 to 0.84 (threshold: 0.05)
New prompt version misses expected structural elements in 3 of 10 cases

subtypes.description: Semantic F1 score has dropped below the regression threshold compared to baseline.
examples: Semantic similarity dropped from 0.88 to 0.78 (threshold: 0.08)
Summary quality degraded in technical domain cases

subtypes.description: Grounding score has dropped below the regression threshold compared to baseline.
examples: Grounding rate dropped from 0.95 to 0.91 (threshold: 0.03)
New retrieval config returns fewer relevant sources

subtypes.description: Latency has increased beyond the regression threshold compared to baseline.
examples: P95 latency increased from 3.2s to 5.1s (threshold: +25%)
Context assembly step now exceeds SLA budget

error_families.description: Errors in memory or context retrieval that degrade downstream AI output quality.

subtypes.description: Retrieved memory or context chunks are not relevant to the current task.
examples: Context bundle includes meeting notes from unrelated project
Similarity search returns chunks with <0.3 cosine similarity

subtypes.description: Relevant memory or context that would have improved grounding or accuracy was not retrieved.
examples: Prior decision log not included despite being referenced in transcript
Relevant regulatory document not retrieved for compliance claim

error_families.description: Errors or signals surfaced through human review processes.

subtypes.description: A human reviewer disagrees with the AI output in a way that requires investigation.
examples: Reviewer rejected extracted decision as inaccurate
Two reviewers reach opposite conclusions on the same output

subtypes.description: Reviewer indicated the output needs additional supporting evidence or context to be actionable.
examples: Reviewer flagged output as 'needs_support' citing insufficient citation
Action item owner cannot be verified without additional context

subtypes.description: Reviewer determined the AI output requires a full rewrite, indicating fundamental quality failure.
examples: Reviewer selected 'rewrite' action for a decision summary
Entire section rejected by domain expert due to factual errors
